items.title: IMF cuts global growth forecast during Hormuz blockade

items.title: Teacher concedes he was 'angry' before 'accidentally' striking student

items.description: With shipments through the Strait of Hormuz stalled after the Iran war began, fertilizer shortages are deepening across India.

items.description: Spain has been one of Europe's loudest voices calling for expanding trade with China and treating it as a strategic ally, rather than an economic and geopolitical rival.

items.title: Trump hosts Dutch royals at the White House for dinner and overnight stay
items.description: King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands were received by US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump at the White House on Monday, 13 April 2026, for a formal dinner and overnight stay as part of their three-day visit to the United States.

items.title: Italy's Meloni suspends defence cooperation deal with Israel
items.description: Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said on Tuesday her government had suspended a defence cooperation deal with Israel, reflecting frayed ties between ​previously close ‌allies as the conflicts in the Middle East continue. Meloni's right-wing government has been one ⁠of Israel's closest friends in Europe, but in recent weeks it has criticised its attacks on Lebanon, which have killed hundreds and injured thousands.

items.description: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

items.description: A case that has shocked Uganda is now taking center stage in court. In Kampala, the trial of Christopher Okello Onyum has officially begun. He stands accused of killing four young children in a crime that has gripped the nation.

items.title: Paris engineer wins Picasso painting at charity raffle
items.description: An art-loving engineer in Paris won a rare Picasso painting worth more than €1 million in a charity raffle, organisers said Tuesday. The draw, livestreamed from Christie's, capped a global fundraiser that sold 120,000 tickets and raised €12 million for Alzheimer’s research.

Italian PM says suspends renewal of defence agreement with Israel

US President Donald Trump criticised Meloni for her unwillingness to help in the Iran war, in an interview published Tuesday. "I'm shocked at her. I thought she had courage, but I was wrong," he said in an interview with Italian daily Corriere della Sera. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports from Rome.

items.title: Antisemitic attacks in 2025 led to highest number of fatalities in 30 years, study finds
items.description: Last year saw the highest level of deadly violence against Jews in over three decades, with 20 people killed in antisemitic attacks, according to an annual study released by Tel Aviv University on Monday. The violence, including a deadly attack at a Hanukkah celebration in Australia, continued a spike that began following Hamas Oct. 7, 2023, attacks and Israel’s subsequent war in Gaza, the report’s authors said.

items.title: Quantum computing: A tech race Europe could win?

items.title: Intimacy coordinator Paloma Garcia Martens on 'respecting people's humanity on set'
items.description: An intimacy coordinator has spoken to FRANCE 24 about how their role is to ensure that everyone's humanity is respected on a film or television set. Paloma Garcia Martens has made a career working on some of Europe's top film and television shows, giving advice primarily to actors and actresses about how to achieve the perfect love scene with someone you probably don't want to be with, while being watched by potentially hundreds of crew. Her book is entitled "L’intimité sur un plateau", or "Intimacy on set". She spoke to us in Perspective.

items.title: MansA: A Parisian home for African arts and culture

As the centre launches a bilingual magazine, we hear from its editor Sebastien Thème on celebrating Black excellence, on the French legislation being debated that should eventually see thousands of looted treasures returned to their countries of origin, and on the cultural figures continuing the work of intellectuals like civil rights activist Angela Davis.

items.title: Global markets slide as Donald Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.description: Global markets have been rattled after Donald Trump announced a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions following failed peace talks with Iran.

items.title: Israel, Lebanon united in liberating Lebanon from Hezbollah, Israeli ambassador to US says
items.description: ⁠The ​Lebanese government made ​it clear during U.S.-brokered talks ​with ‌Israel ⁠that ‌they no longer want to ⁠be "occupied" by Hezbollah and ​that ‌there were conversations about long-term ‌vision for ​clearly delineated border, Israel's ambassador to the ​United States ​Yechiel ​Leiter told reporters on ​Tuesday. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
items.description: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

items.title: Israeli fire kills three in Gaza following new ceasefire talks
items.description: Medics said the strike had hit a group of men outside a school in Deir al-Balah in the central Gaza Strip. There was no immediate comment by the Israeli military

items.title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates historic Mass at rain‑soaked Basilica in Algiers
items.description: Algeria hosted Pope Leo XIV on Monday 14 April for a Mass at the Basilica of Our Lady of Africa in Algiers, marking the first papal visit to the country.

items.title: Halliburton wins multi-billion-dollar YPF fracking contract in Argentina
items.description: Halliburton says it has secured a multibillion-dollar contract from Argentina’s state-run oil company YPF to provide bundled fracking services at Vaca Muerta. Leer más

items.title: News24 | PHOTOS | Prince Harry and Meghan visit Australia in first trip since royal rift
items.description: Prince Harry and Meghan visit Australia for the first time since their royal split.

items.title: Israel vows to fight on as Trump grants Iran ceasefire
items.description: United States and Iran agree a ceasefire and are due to launch talks in Pakistan aimed at reaching a longer-term deal, but crucial sticking points remain. Leer más

items.title: Senegal issues first conviction under tougher homosexuality law
items.description: Senegal has issued the first conviction under a new law increasing the punishment for homosexuality.

items.title: Spanish PM Sanchez' wife charged with corruption
items.description: The case is based on a complaint by an anti-corruption group with far-right ties, and is the latest in a string of corruption allegations which have plagued Sanchez' inner circle.

items.title: News24 | Britney Spears enters rehab facility following arrest amid ongoing personal struggles
items.description: US pop superstar Britney Spears has checked herself into rehab following her arrest last month on suspicion of driving while intoxicated near Los Angeles.

items.title: Ukraine hopes for more cooperation after Hungary elects new prime minister
items.description: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.

items.title: News24 | Furious Iran threatens Gulf neighbours as US blocks ports, oil prices dip below $100
items.description: The US military began a blockade of Iran’s ports, angering Tehran and adding uncertainty around the crucial waterway, although hopes for dialogue to end the war provided some relief to oil markets, where benchmark prices fell below $100.

items.title: Cameroon's Catholics fear papal visit could pay political dividends for President Biya
items.description: As rumours emerged of a papal visit, some of Cameroon's Catholics voiced fears the trip would give longtime President Paul Biya a chance to polish his image six months on from deadly protests following his contested re-election.

items.title: Argentina’s Congress to debate easing glacier protection law to boost mining
items.description: Activists and demonstrators are set to take to the streets later today in protest at debate over changes to Argentina's National Glacier Law. Leer más

items.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
items.description: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

items.title: News24 | Don’t want to get into ‘debate with him’: How Trump’s feud with Pope on war could backfire
items.description: Pope Leo told Reuters that he plans to continue speaking out against war after US ‌President Donald Trump’s direct attack on the leader of the 1.4-billion-member church.

items.title: Man who sent threatening letters was 'drinking himself to sleep'
items.description: A man who sent threatening letters to a Sydney mosque was "drinking himself to sleep" and "fell into some type of internet rabbit hole", a court hears.

items.title: French woman, 86, held by ICE after moving to US to reunite with long-lost love
items.description: The son of pensioner Marie-Thérèse says he worries for his mother's frail health, while she is detained in a Louisiana ICE centre.

items.title: South Africa returns looted human remains and sacred carving to Zimbabwe
items.description: South Africa on Tuesday (14 April) handed back to Zimbabwe ancestral human remains and a centuries-old stone carving of its sacred national emblem, the Zimbabwe bird.

items.title: China says policy to improve relations with India remains unchanged
items.description: China has been publishing its names for various regions in Arunachal Pradesh since 2017 for which India has been consistently objecting, saying that “fictitious names" to Indian territory cannot alter "undeniable reality"

items.title: Argentine author Samanta Schweblin wins one-million-euro literary prize
items.description: Argentine author takes inaugural €1m award for 'El buen mal'; Controversy surrounds state-backed Aena’s entry into the literary sphere with Premio Aena de Narrativa Hispanoamericana prize. Leer más

items.title: Why India walked away from its bid to host COP33
items.description: India has quietly abandoned its bid to host the UN's top-tier climate conference COP33, marking a shift from PM Narendra Modi's pledge in 2023. Experts and analysts explore what's behind the decision.

items.title: Cabinet chiefs past and present
items.description: The Cabinet chief’s role as whipping boy can be traced back to the post’s origins in the 1995 constitutional reform. Leer más

items.title: Did the ayatollahs get one over on Trump?
items.description: Even though Trump is desperately looking for an excuse to call it quits, he could change his mind yet again when it dawns on him that the remnants of the Iranian regime are making him look like a loser. Leer más

items.title: Amazon to buy satellite firm Globalstar in $11.57 billion deal to take on Musk's Starlink
World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
items.description: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guinness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

items.title: China terms U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz 'dangerous and irresponsible' move
items.description: Beijing also denied aiding Iran militarily and threatened to hit back if U.S. President Donald Trump hikes tariffs against China on the allegation of helping Tehran

items.title: Collard handed monster ban over 'entirely unacceptable' homophobic slur
items.description: St Kilda forward Lance Collard is suspended after a marathon AFL Tribunal hearing with a deliberation of over 90 minutes.

items.title: Another woman accuses Swalwell of drugging and raping her in 2018
items.description: Lonna Drewes accused Swalwell of drugging her drink before assaulting her in a hotel room.

items.title: Video: UN chief Antonio Guterres says talks on Iran war likely to restart
items.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres says it’s “highly probably” talks to end the US-Israel war on Iran will resume.

items.title: Milei pleads for patience as growth slows and approval falls
items.description: “We know that the last months were hard. That’s why we’re asking for patience. This is the right path," says President Milei in social media post. Leer más

items.title: U.K. inquiry lays bare 'catastrophic' missed chances before stabbings at dance class
items.description: An inquiry found that a mass killing by a British teenager in 2024 at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class could have been prevented if his parents and state agencies had acted on his violence fixation.

items.title: U.S., Iran may meet again in Islamabad for second round of talks by next week: Report
items.description: The contacts are ongoing for the revival of stalled negotiations between the U.S. and Iran, and talks may be held again before the end of the two-week ceasefire on April 21, says reports citing highly placed sources

items.title: Can this man broaden the appeal of a South African party seen by some as 'too white'?
items.description: The Democratic Alliance's new leader, Geordin Hill-Lewis, admits the party's support is too narrow.

items.title: Russia offers to take in Iran's enriched uranium
items.description: Failed talks between Iran and the United States over the weekend dashed hopes of a swift deal to permanently end the war that has killed thousands and thrown the global economy into turmoil since it began in late February

items.title: Milei’s auction favours group with past China ties, US dredger warns
items.description: US dredging company calls out bid terms of President Javier Milei’s signature concession in Argentina, claiming they’re tilted in favour of a consortium with past ties to China. Leer más

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as fuel costs hit tourism
items.description: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

items.title: Court hears conflicting accounts over fatal blow in Gold Coast assault case
items.description: A court has heard conflicting witness accounts of an alleged Gold Coast assault that left 22-year-old Cameron Duce dead.

items.title: Prosecutors probe US$10,000 New York flights linked to Cabinet Chief Adorni
items.description: Investigation is seeking to establish who paid for the return journey of Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni and his wife, Bettina Angeletti, from New York after Argentina Week roadshow. Leer más

items.title: The iceman goeth
items.description: Resources are the name of the game in today’s world and the amendments to the 2010 Glaciers Law should be seen within that context. Leer más

items.title: Germany marks 69th birthday of zoo’s oldest gorilla
items.description: Berlin Zoo celebrates Fatou, a 69‑year‑old western lowland gorilla, highlighting how specialised care in captivity can extend the lives of endangered species.

items.title: News24 | Third Iran-linked tanker enters Strait of Hormuz
items.description: A third Iran-linked tanker was entering the Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, on the first full day of ‌the US blockade on vessels calling at Iranian ports, shipping data showed.

items.title: Authorities in Argentina say fatal school shooting linked online ‘TCC’ crime community
items.description: Security Minister Alejandra Monteoliva says San Cristóbal school shooting was not “an isolated case nor a case of bullying,” claiming the shooter “participated in an international digital network” and Internet subculture dedicated to such incidents. Leer más

items.title: Billionaire's 'slow moving car crash' leads to individuals being investigated by ASIC
items.description: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

items.title: Burundi court partially acquits jailed journalist after two years
items.description: A Burundian court partially acquitted journalist Sandra Muhoza on Monday after nearly two years behind bars.

items.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
items.description: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

items.title: Argentina to play Honduras and Iceland in pre-World Cup friendlies
items.description: 'La Scaloneta' will face Honduras on June 6 in Texas and Iceland’s senior side on June 9 in Alabama ahead of World Cup kick-off. Leer más

items.title: Lula asks US to send ex-intelligence chief to Brazil after ICE arrest
items.description: Brazil's president says he expects Alexandre Ramagem to be returned to Brazil to serve prison sentence there.

items.title: Inflation in Argentina continues to accelerate: consumer prices rose 3.4% in March
items.description: Highest monthly increase since March 2025; Prices rose 3.4% last month and have risen 32.6% over last 12 months. Leer más

items.title: Historic but not enough? Colombia’s Gustavo Petro defends cocaine seizures
items.description: Colombia's president has intercepted historic volumes of illicit cocaine. But will it be enough to lessen US pressure?

items.title: CAF chief open to corruption probe as Senegal-Morocco row deepens
items.description: Patrice Motsepe says he welcomes any independent inquiry into governance at the African football body, as tensions rise over the decision to strip Senegal of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations and award it to Morocco.

items.title: What will it take? Israel-Lebanon talks open as fighting continues
items.description: Israeli and Lebanese envoys appearing together on camera in the same room, that may well be a first. It's happening under the auspices of the US Secretary of State in Washington. Now if you’re rooting for peace… there's good news and bad news. The good news is that the Lebanese government’s never been so vocal in calling on Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah to disarm… and the US president has a real stake in reining in Israel if he wants his attempts to strike a deal with Tehran to bear fruits.

items.title: Inquiry told school to lose students if $1.5-billion waste incinerator built
items.description: A school in the middle of a $22-million master plan for its growing enrolment numbers is worried the buildings will not be needed if a waste to energy plant is approved in the town.

items.description: A selection of the stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Partner of US influencer who died in Zanzibar speaking to police as witness
items.description: The police said Ashly Robinson, known online as Ashlee Jenae, had died after attempting to take her own life.
